texto: After all, one travels in order for things to happen and change; otherwise you might as well stay at home.
explicacao: Citação de Nicolas Bouvier sobre temas ligados a sabedoria. “After all, one travels in order for things to happen and change; otherwise you might as well stay…” articula sabedoria, travel, travel-writing em registro de sabedoria, com relevância para contextos como mudanca e viagem.

frase_original: After all, one travels in order for things to happen and change; otherwise you might as well stay at home.

texto: That day, I really believed that I had grasped something and that henceforth my life would be changed. But insights cannot be held for ever. Like water, the world ripples across you and for a while you take on its colours. Then it recedes, and leaves you face to face with the void you carry inside yourself, confronting that central inadequacy of soul which you must learn to rub shoulders with and to combat, and which, paradoxically, may be our surest impetus.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Nicolas Bouvier: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “That day, I really believed that I had grasped something and that henceforth my life would be cha…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, travel, which pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de mudanca e viagem.

texto: Traveling outgrows its motives. It soon proves sufficient in itself. You think you are making a trip, but soon it is making you - or unmaking you.
explicacao: Citação de Nicolas Bouvier sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Traveling outgrows its motives. It soon proves sufficient in itself. You think you are making a t…” articula reflexao, travel, making em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como viagem e reflexao.
